title: Affect Labeling Review — Screening Log
description: Schema for any per-record screening log accompanying the affect-labeling systematic review. Accepts both canonical hand-screened logs (with explicit decisions and exclusion reasons) and derived logs (with binary inclusion labels only).

record_id.description: Stable identifier for this record within the screening log. Recommend a prefix like 'pub-' for PubMed-derived rows.

source_database.description: Database of origin for this record.

abstract.description: Article abstract. May be null if not retrievable or omitted for copyright reasons.

authors.description: Author list, semicolon-separated, in citation order.

decision.description: Final screening decision. For hand-screened logs: 'include' / 'exclude' / 'maybe' (escalates to a second reviewer). For derived logs (e.g., derived_screening_log.csv): 'include' if the row matched a canonical-identifiable include; 'unknown' for unmatched rows that may be true excludes or unidentified canonical includes (positive-unlabeled framing). 'unscreened' is reserved for rows queued but not yet decided.

decision_stage.description: Which screening stage produced the decision. 'combined' for derived logs that don't preserve the stage distinction.

exclusion_reason.description: Reason for exclusion, drawn from the codebook in data/exclusion_reason_codebook.md. Null if decision is 'include' or 'maybe' or 'unscreened'.

synthesis_subset.description: Subset of the synthesis that an included paper belongs to. Null if the paper was excluded.

screener_id.description: Identifier of the human (or pipeline) that made the decision. Use 'derived_pubmed_2026' for the derived screening log.

screening_date.description: ISO-8601 date the decision was recorded.

notes.description: Free-text notes; e.g., reasoning for borderline decisions.

examples.title: Putting feelings into words: Affect labeling disrupts amygdala activity in response to affective stimuli
examples.authors: Lieberman MD; Eisenberger NI; Crockett MJ; Tom SM; Pfeifer JH; Way BM
examples.notes: Foundational paper for the AL paradigm.

examples.title: Example excluded paper title
examples.notes: Derived label: not in manuscript's included-papers list.
